evals.name: basic-feature-creation
evals.prompt: Wir haben gerade ein Design fuer ein Benutzer-Login-System besprochen und abgenommen. Die Anforderungen sind: Benutzer koennen sich mit Email und Passwort anmelden, nach 3 Fehlversuchen wird das Konto gesperrt, es gibt eine Passwort-vergessen Funktion per Email. Erstelle bitte die Feature Files dafuer.
evals.expected_output: Gherkin .feature Dateien mit Given/When/Then Szenarien fuer Login, Kontosperrung und Passwort-Reset.

assertions.name: skill_invoked
assertions.text: The feature-design skill was invoked

assertions.name: feature_files_created
assertions.text: At least one .feature file was written to the filesystem

assertions.name: valid_gherkin
assertions.text: Output contains valid Gherkin: Feature:, Scenario:, Given, When, Then

assertions.name: happy_path
assertions.text: A scenario for successful login exists

assertions.name: error_cases
assertions.text: At least 2 error/edge case scenarios exist (wrong password, locked account, expired link, etc.)

assertions.name: lockout_rule
assertions.text: A scenario for account lockout after 3 failed attempts exists

assertions.name: password_reset
assertions.text: A scenario for password reset via email exists

assertions.name: declarative_style
assertions.text: Scenarios are declarative (WHAT not HOW): no CSS selectors, no HTTP verbs, no click-by-click UI steps

assertions.name: ears_mapping
assertions.text: Requirements were analyzed or mapped using structured patterns (EARS or similar) before writing scenarios

assertions.name: tags_used
assertions.text: Scenarios use tags for organization (@critical, @edge-case, @smoke or similar)

assertions.name: scenario_outline
assertions.text: Scenario Outline with Examples table is used where parameterization makes sense (e.g., different login failure reasons)

assertions.name: self_review
assertions.text: A self-review of coverage, clarity, independence, boundaries, language was performed

assertions.name: returns_to_brainstorming
assertions.text: The skill does NOT invoke writing-plans directly. It returns control to brainstorming for spec writing.

evals.name: conflict-with-existing-features
evals.prompt: Wir haben ein bestehendes Projekt mit einer toolbar.feature die ein Szenario hat: 'Scenario: Auto-Layout anwenden / Given die Knoten sind unuebersichtlich positioniert / When ich den Auto-Layout-Button klicke / Then werden die Knoten automatisch uebersichtlich angeordnet'. Jetzt wollen wir ein neues Feature: statt einem Button soll es ein Dropdown-Menue geben mit verschiedenen Layout-Algorithmen (kraftbasiert, hierarchisch, kreisfoermig). Erstelle die Feature Files fuer das neue Layout-System.
evals.expected_output: Konflikterkennung, User-Approval abwarten, Change Impact Cascade dokumentieren.

files.content:
Feature: Toolbar
  Die Toolbar bietet Schnellzugriff auf wichtige Aktionen.

  Background:
    Given ein Wissensgraph mit Knoten und Kanten ist geoeffnet

  @critical
  Scenario: Auto-Layout anwenden
    Given die Knoten sind unuebersichtlich positioniert
    When ich den "Auto-Layout"-Button klicke
    Then werden die Knoten automatisch uebersichtlich angeordnet

  Scenario: Zaehler zeigt aktuelle Anzahl
    Then zeigt der Zaehler die korrekte Anzahl Knoten an

assertions.name: skill_invoked
assertions.text: The feature-design skill was invoked

assertions.name: existing_files_read
assertions.text: ALL existing .feature files were read before writing new scenarios

assertions.name: conflict_identified
assertions.text: The conflict between Auto-Layout button and new dropdown is explicitly identified and reported

assertions.name: user_approval_requested
assertions.text: The agent ASKS the user how to resolve the conflict and WAITS for a response before modifying toolbar.feature

assertions.name: no_silent_modification
assertions.text: toolbar.feature is NOT modified without the user having explicitly approved the change

assertions.name: change_impact_documented
assertions.text: The change impact cascade is documented: which step definitions break, which code needs updating

assertions.name: new_scenarios_planned
assertions.text: New Gherkin scenarios for the dropdown with 3 layout algorithms are planned or created (creation may be blocked until user approves conflict resolution)

assertions.name: non_conflicting_preserved
assertions.text: The non-conflicting Zaehler scenario is identified as preserved unchanged

assertions.name: edge_cases_planned
assertions.text: Edge cases are planned or created (empty graph, single node, etc.)

evals.name: quality-review-process
evals.prompt: Hier sind die Anforderungen fuer einen Schnellfilter: Benutzer koennen Knoten nach Label filtern, der Filter ist ein Textfeld in der Toolbar, beim Tippen werden nicht-matchende Knoten ausgegraut, bei leerem Filter werden alle Knoten wieder normal angezeigt. Bitte erstelle Feature Files.
evals.expected_output: Hochwertige Feature Files mit Self-Review und Quality Review.

assertions.name: skill_invoked
assertions.text: The feature-design skill was invoked

assertions.name: feature_files_created
assertions.text: At least one .feature file was written to the filesystem

assertions.name: declarative_style
assertions.text: Scenarios use declarative domain language, not imperative UI steps

assertions.name: domain_language
assertions.text: Domain terms used consistently (Knoten, Label, Filter) — no technical jargon (DOM, CSS, event handler)

assertions.name: empty_filter
assertions.text: Scenario for empty filter (all nodes visible) exists

assertions.name: no_match
assertions.text: Scenario for no matching nodes exists

assertions.name: partial_match
assertions.text: Scenario for partial text matching exists

assertions.name: background_used
assertions.text: Background section used for shared preconditions across scenarios

assertions.name: self_review_performed
assertions.text: Self-review was performed checking coverage, clarity, independence, boundaries, language

assertions.name: quality_review_performed
assertions.text: A fresh-agent quality review was performed and found or confirmed quality aspects

assertions.name: issues_found_and_fixed
assertions.text: If the review found issues (conjunctive scenarios, missing edge cases, etc.), they were fixed before presenting to user

evals.name: architecture-aware-scenarios
evals.prompt: Wir haben eine architecture.md die Performance als kritische Charakteristik definiert (API Response <200ms p95) und Modularity als wichtig. Jetzt sollen Feature Files fuer eine Suchfunktion erstellt werden: Benutzer geben einen Suchbegriff ein, das System durchsucht alle Knoten-Labels und Metadaten-Felder, Ergebnisse werden sofort angezeigt. Beruecksichtige die Architektur-Anforderungen in den Szenarien.
evals.expected_output: Feature Files die Performance-Anforderungen aus architecture.md in Szenarien reflektieren.

files.content:
# Architecture Characteristics

## Top 3 Priority Characteristics
1. Performance — API <200ms p95
2. Modularity — No circular dependencies
3. Testability — >80% coverage

## All Characteristics
### Operational
| Characteristic | Priority | Concrete Goal | Fitness Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| Performance | Critical | API <200ms p95 | Yes - load test |
| Availability | Nice-to-have | 99% uptime | No |

### Structural
| Characteristic | Priority | Concrete Goal | Fitness Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| Modularity | Important | No circular deps | Yes - dependency check |
| Testability | Important | >80% coverage | Yes - coverage gate |

assertions.name: skill_invoked
assertions.text: The feature-design skill was invoked

assertions.name: architecture_read
assertions.text: The architecture.md was read and its characteristics acknowledged

assertions.name: performance_scenario
assertions.text: At least one scenario references or reflects the performance requirement (e.g., results appear immediately, response within acceptable time)

assertions.name: search_happy_path
assertions.text: Scenario for successful search with matching results exists

assertions.name: search_no_results
assertions.text: Scenario for search with no matching results exists

assertions.name: search_edge_cases
assertions.text: Edge cases covered (empty search, special characters, very long input)

assertions.name: advisory_offered
assertions.text: The advisory extension point was acknowledged or offered (architecture advisor, etc.)

evals.name: immutability-under-pressure
evals.prompt: Wir implementieren gerade ein neues Feature und der Agent hat festgestellt, dass die bestehende navigation.feature nicht ganz zum neuen Design passt. Die navigation.feature sagt 'When ich auf einen Knoten doppelklicke / Then oeffnet sich das Bearbeitungsfenster' aber unser neues Design will, dass Doppelklick den Knoten umbenennt statt ein Fenster zu oeffnen. Passe die Feature Files an.
evals.expected_output: Der Skill erkennt, dass dies eine Anforderungsaenderung ist und fragt den User bevor er die bestehende Feature File aendert.

files.content:
Feature: Navigation
  Benutzer koennen im Wissensgraph navigieren.

  Background:
    Given ein Wissensgraph mit mehreren Knoten ist geoeffnet

  @critical
  Scenario: Knoten per Doppelklick bearbeiten
    Given der Knoten "Server" ist sichtbar
    When ich auf den Knoten "Server" doppelklicke
    Then oeffnet sich das Bearbeitungsfenster fuer "Server"

  Scenario: Zoom per Mausrad
    When ich das Mausrad nach vorne drehe
    Then wird der Graph vergroessert dargestellt

assertions.name: skill_invoked
assertions.text: The feature-design skill was invoked

assertions.name: conflict_detected
assertions.text: The conflict with the existing double-click behavior is identified

assertions.name: user_asked_before_change
assertions.text: The agent asks the user for approval BEFORE modifying navigation.feature — does NOT just change it

assertions.name: change_reason_documented
assertions.text: If changes are made, the reason is documented (comment in feature file or in transcript)

assertions.name: zoom_scenario_preserved
assertions.text: The non-conflicting Zoom scenario is preserved unchanged

assertions.name: no_rationalization
assertions.text: The agent does NOT rationalize past the approval requirement (e.g., 'since this is clearly needed...' or 'proceeding as this is straightforward')

evals.name: complex-multi-feature-requirements
evals.prompt: Wir bauen ein Cluster-System fuer unseren Wissensgraph. Die Anforderungen: 1) Automatische Cluster-Erkennung basierend auf Kantenverbindungen, 2) Visuelle Darstellung von Clustern mit farbigen Rahmen, 3) Cluster koennen manuell zusammengefasst/aufgeloest werden, 4) Cluster-Zuordnung wird beim Speichern persistiert, 5) Cluster-spezifische Layout-Algorithmen. Erstelle die Feature Files — das sind mehrere zusammenhaengende Features.
evals.expected_output: Mehrere .feature Files organisiert nach Domain-Konzept, nicht eine riesige Datei.

assertions.name: skill_invoked
assertions.text: The feature-design skill was invoked

assertions.name: multiple_feature_files
assertions.text: Multiple .feature files were created, organized by domain concept (not one giant file)

assertions.name: cluster_detection
assertions.text: Scenarios for automatic cluster detection exist

assertions.name: cluster_visualization
assertions.text: Scenarios for visual cluster representation exist

assertions.name: cluster_persistence
assertions.text: Scenarios for saving/loading cluster data exist

assertions.name: manual_operations
assertions.text: Scenarios for manual cluster merge/dissolve exist

assertions.name: one_behavior_per_scenario
assertions.text: Each scenario tests exactly one behavior — no conjunctive scenarios

assertions.name: completeness
assertions.text: All 5 requirement areas have at least one scenario each

assertions.name: declarative
assertions.text: Scenarios describe behavior, not implementation (no API calls, no database operations)
